evals.name: missing-credentials-setup-flow
evals.prompt: Show me all my Leantime projects

files.description: Empty project directory (no .env) — simulates fresh setup
files.note: Test runner should execute in a temp directory with no .env present

evals.expected_behavior: Run scripts/check_connection.py → exit 1 (missing credentials) → direct user to references/setup.md for provisioning steps. Do NOT attempt any JSON-RPC calls without valid credentials.

assertions.description: Claude runs scripts/check_connection.py before any API call
assertions.check: check_connection.py appears before any curl or API call

assertions.description: No JSON-RPC request is made when credentials are missing
assertions.check: no curl or JSON-RPC call appears after probe failure

assertions.description: references/setup.md is loaded to guide the user through credential setup
assertions.check: setup.md is referenced or its contents are used in the response

evals.name: list-projects
evals.expected_behavior: Run scripts/check_connection.py → load references/projects.md → execute leantime.rpc.projects.getAll via curl → format and return project list.

assertions.description: Claude runs scripts/check_connection.py as the very first action
assertions.check: check_connection.py appears before any JSON-RPC call

assertions.description: references/projects.md is loaded before the API call
assertions.check: projects.md is accessed before getAll is called

assertions.description: Only the projects reference is loaded
assertions.check: tickets.md, users.md, comments.md are NOT loaded

assertions.description: Method leantime.rpc.projects.getAll is used
assertions.check: curl body contains leantime.rpc.projects.getAll

evals.name: create-ticket
evals.prompt: Create a bug ticket in project 42: the login page is broken on Safari
evals.expected_behavior: Run scripts/check_connection.py → load references/tickets.md → execute leantime.rpc.tickets.addTicket with headline, type=bug, projectId=42 → confirm creation with returned ticket ID.

assertions.description: Claude runs scripts/check_connection.py before any API call
assertions.check: check_connection.py appears before addTicket call

assertions.description: references/tickets.md is loaded
assertions.check: tickets.md is accessed before the API call

assertions.description: Method leantime.rpc.tickets.addTicket is used
assertions.check: curl body contains leantime.rpc.tickets.addTicket

assertions.description: projectId 42 is included in the request
assertions.check: params contain projectId: '42'

assertions.description: type is set to bug
assertions.check: params contain type: 'bug' or equivalent

evals.name: get-user
evals.expected_behavior: Run scripts/check_connection.py → load references/users.md → execute leantime.rpc.users.getUser with id=7 → display user details.

assertions.description: Claude runs scripts/check_connection.py before any API call
assertions.check: check_connection.py appears before getUser call

assertions.description: references/users.md is loaded
assertions.check: users.md is accessed before the API call

assertions.description: Method leantime.rpc.users.getUser is used with id=7
assertions.check: curl body contains leantime.rpc.users.getUser and id: '7'

evals.name: add-comment
evals.prompt: Add a comment 'fixed in PR #42' to ticket 17 in Leantime
evals.expected_behavior: Run scripts/check_connection.py → load references/comments.md → execute leantime.rpc.tickets.addComment with text and moduleId=17 → confirm comment added.

assertions.description: Claude runs scripts/check_connection.py before any API call
assertions.check: check_connection.py appears before addComment call

assertions.description: references/comments.md is loaded
assertions.check: comments.md is accessed before the API call

assertions.description: Method leantime.rpc.tickets.addComment is used
assertions.check: curl body contains leantime.rpc.tickets.addComment

assertions.description: moduleId 17 is included in the request
assertions.check: params contain moduleId: '17'

evals.name: out-of-scope-time-tracking
evals.prompt: Log 3 hours of time on ticket 42 in Leantime
evals.expected_behavior: Skill recognises time tracking as out of scope for v1 and refuses politely. Does NOT attempt to call any timesheets or time-tracking RPC methods. Directs user to manage time tracking directly in the Leantime UI.

assertions.description: Claude refuses to log time and explains it is out of scope
assertions.check: response contains 'out of scope' or 'not supported in v1' or equivalent

assertions.description: No time tracking API call is made
assertions.check: no curl call with timesheet, timelog, or time-related method name

evals.name: out-of-scope-delete-ticket
evals.expected_behavior: Skill refuses — delete operations are out of scope in v1. Does NOT call deleteTicket or any DELETE-equivalent method. Explains the limitation and suggests archiving or closing the ticket instead.

assertions.description: Claude refuses to delete the ticket
assertions.check: response explains delete is out of scope or not supported

assertions.description: No deleteTicket or equivalent method is called
assertions.check: no curl body contains delete or remove in the method name

assertions.description: Claude suggests closing or archiving the ticket as an alternative
assertions.check: response mentions status update or Leantime UI as alternatives

evals.name: out-of-scope-create-user
evals.prompt: Create a new user account in Leantime for alice@example.com
evals.expected_behavior: Skill refuses — user creation is out of scope in v1 and listed as read-only. Does NOT call addUser. Directs user to Leantime admin panel at Settings → Users.

assertions.description: Claude refuses to create a user and explains it is out of scope
assertions.check: response explains user creation is not supported in v1

assertions.description: No addUser or createUser method is called
assertions.check: no curl body contains addUser, createUser, or equivalent

assertions.description: Claude directs user to the Leantime admin interface
assertions.check: response references Settings → Users or the admin panel
